selection_method.note: The CVE set is the union of all CVE IDs listed on Debian's giflib source-package tracker page as of 2026-04-02, then enriched with the full CVE Services record for each ID and local source-tree mentions.

tracker.name: CVE-2005-2974
tracker.description: libungif library before 4.1.0 allows attackers to cause a denial of service via a crafted GIF file that triggers a null dereference.
tracker.source_text: CVE (at NVD; CERT, ENISA, LWN, oss-sec, fulldisc, Debian ELTS, Red Hat, Ubuntu, Gentoo, SUSE bugzilla/CVE, GitHub advisories/code/issues, web search, more)
notes: Just a bug, hardly security implications

descriptions.value: libungif library before 4.1.0 allows attackers to cause a denial of service via a crafted GIF file that triggers a null dereference.

local_source_mentions.text: * Fixes applied for CVE-2005-2974 and CVE-2005-3350
* Adding patch from libungif to fix CVE-2005-2974 and CVE-2005-3350.
- isn't affected by CVE-2005-2974 and CVE-2005-3350 (Closes: #395382).

cves.scope_note: Legacy libungif-lineage issue carried on Debian's giflib source-package tracker; Debian notes giflib 4.1.4-1 and later as fixed.

tracker.name: CVE-2005-3350
tracker.description: libungif library before 4.1.0 allows attackers to corrupt memory and possibly execute arbitrary code via a crafted GIF file that leads to an out-of-bounds write.
tracker.source_text: CVE (at NVD; CERT, ENISA, LWN, oss-sec, fulldisc, Debian ELTS, Red Hat, Ubuntu, Gentoo, SUSE bugzilla/CVE, GitHub advisories/code/issues, web search, more)

descriptions.value: libungif library before 4.1.0 allows attackers to corrupt memory and possibly execute arbitrary code via a crafted GIF file that leads to an out-of-bounds write.

local_source_mentions.text: * Fixes applied for CVE-2005-2974 and CVE-2005-3350
* Adding patch from libungif to fix CVE-2005-2974 and CVE-2005-3350.
- isn't affected by CVE-2005-2974 and CVE-2005-3350 (Closes: #395382).

cves.scope_note: Legacy libungif-lineage issue carried on Debian's giflib source-package tracker; Debian notes giflib 4.1.4-1 and later as fixed.

tracker.name: CVE-2015-7555
tracker.description: Heap-based buffer overflow in giffix.c in giffix in giflib 5.1.1 allows attackers to cause a denial of service (program crash) via crafted image and logical screen width fields in a GIF file.
tracker.source_text: CVE (at NVD; CERT, ENISA, LWN, oss-sec, fulldisc, Debian ELTS, Red Hat, Ubuntu, Gentoo, SUSE bugzilla/CVE, GitHub advisories/code/issues, web search, more)

descriptions.value: Heap-based buffer overflow in giffix.c in giffix in giflib 5.1.1 allows attackers to cause a denial of service (program crash) via crafted image and logical screen width fields in a GIF file.

local_source_mentions.text: - CVE-2015-7555, Heap-based buffer overflow in giffix utility.

tracker.name: CVE-2016-3177
tracker.description: Multiple use-after-free and double-free vulnerabilities in gifcolor.c in GIFLIB 5.1.2 have unspecified impact and attack vectors.
tracker.source_text: CVE (at NVD; CERT, ENISA, LWN, oss-sec, fulldisc, Debian ELTS, Red Hat, Ubuntu, Gentoo, SUSE bugzilla/CVE, GitHub advisories/code/issues, web search, more)
notes: Issue only in gifcolor utility, not installed into giflib-tools
Issue introduced upstream in 5.1.2 and fixed in 5.1.3.

descriptions.value: Multiple use-after-free and double-free vulnerabilities in gifcolor.c in GIFLIB 5.1.2 have unspecified impact and attack vectors.

references.name: [oss-security] 20160316 Re: CVE Request : Use-after-free in gifcolor

tracker.name: CVE-2016-3977
tracker.description: Heap-based buffer overflow in util/gif2rgb.c in gif2rgb in giflib 5.1.2 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) via the background color index in a GIF file.
tracker.source_text: CVE (at NVD; CERT, ENISA, LWN, oss-sec, fulldisc, Debian ELTS, Red Hat, Ubuntu, Gentoo, SUSE bugzilla/CVE, GitHub advisories/code/issues, web search, more)
notes: The issue was originally fixed in 5.1.4-0.3 but then the NMU upload
5.1.4-0.4 just dropped the patch claiming the patch was already present
which is untrue and reopening the issue.

descriptions.value: Heap-based buffer overflow in util/gif2rgb.c in gif2rgb in giflib 5.1.2 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) via the background color index in a GIF file.

description_data.value: Heap-based buffer overflow in util/gif2rgb.c in gif2rgb in giflib 5.1.2 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) via the background color index in a GIF file.

local_source_mentions.text: - Drop '03-spelling_fixes.patch' and 'CVE-2016-3977.patch';
+ Drop CVE-2016-3977.patch, included upstream.
* Heap-based buffer overflow in util/gif2rgb.c (CVE-2016-3977)
* CVE-2016-3977: gif2rgb: heap buffer overflow. Closes: #820526.
* Security issues already fixed in 5.1.2: CVE-2016-3977.

tracker.name: CVE-2018-11489
tracker.description: The DGifDecompressLine function in dgif_lib.c in GIFLIB (possibly version 3.0.x), as later shipped in cgif.c in sam2p 0.49.4, has a heap-based buffer overflow because a certain CrntCode array index is not checked. This will lead to a denial of service or possibly unspecified other impact.
tracker.source_text: CVE (at NVD; CERT, ENISA, LWN, oss-sec, fulldisc, Debian ELTS, Red Hat, Ubuntu, Gentoo, SUSE bugzilla/CVE, GitHub advisories/code/issues, web search, more)
notes: Issue was reported against sam2p but issue is in dgif_lib.c from giflib.

descriptions.value: The DGifDecompressLine function in dgif_lib.c in GIFLIB (possibly version 3.0.x), as later shipped in cgif.c in sam2p 0.49.4, has a heap-based buffer overflow because a certain CrntCode array index is not checked. This will lead to a denial of service or possibly unspecified other impact.

description_data.value: The DGifDecompressLine function in dgif_lib.c in GIFLIB (possibly version 3.0.x), as later shipped in cgif.c in sam2p 0.49.4, has a heap-based buffer overflow because a certain CrntCode array index is not checked. This will lead to a denial of service or possibly unspecified other impact.

tracker.name: CVE-2018-11490
tracker.description: The DGifDecompressLine function in dgif_lib.c in GIFLIB (possibly version 3.0.x), as later shipped in cgif.c in sam2p 0.49.4, has a heap-based buffer overflow because a certain "Private->RunningCode - 2" array index is not checked. This will lead to a denial of service or possibly unspecified other impact.
tracker.source_text: CVE (at NVD; CERT, ENISA, LWN, oss-sec, fulldisc, Debian ELTS, Red Hat, Ubuntu, Gentoo, SUSE bugzilla/CVE, GitHub advisories/code/issues, web search, more)
notes: Issue was reported against sam2p but issue is in dgif_lib.c from giflib.

descriptions.value: The DGifDecompressLine function in dgif_lib.c in GIFLIB (possibly version 3.0.x), as later shipped in cgif.c in sam2p 0.49.4, has a heap-based buffer overflow because a certain "Private->RunningCode - 2" array index is not checked. This will lead to a denial of service or possibly unspecified other impact.

local_source_mentions.text: in cgif.c.  This had been assigned CVE-2018-11490.

tracker.name: CVE-2019-15133
tracker.description: In GIFLIB before 2019-02-16, a malformed GIF file triggers a divide-by-zero exception in the decoder function DGifSlurp in dgif_lib.c if the height field of the ImageSize data structure is equal to zero.
tracker.source_text: CVE (at NVD; CERT, ENISA, LWN, oss-sec, fulldisc, Debian ELTS, Red Hat, Ubuntu, Gentoo, SUSE bugzilla/CVE, GitHub advisories/code/issues, web search, more)

descriptions.value: In GIFLIB before 2019-02-16, a malformed GIF file triggers a divide-by-zero exception in the decoder function DGifSlurp in dgif_lib.c if the height field of the ImageSize data structure is equal to zero.

tracker.name: CVE-2020-23922
tracker.description: An issue was discovered in giflib through 5.1.4. DumpScreen2RGB in gif2rgb.c has a heap-based buffer over-read.
tracker.source_text: CVE (at NVD; CERT, ENISA, LWN, oss-sec, fulldisc, Debian ELTS, Red Hat, Ubuntu, Gentoo, SUSE bugzilla/CVE, GitHub advisories/code/issues, web search, more)
notes: Specific to gif2rgb. Crash in CLI tool, no security impact
Reproducer does not trigger using giflib 5.2.1-2.5 with asan or valgrind.

descriptions.value: An issue was discovered in giflib through 5.1.4. DumpScreen2RGB in gif2rgb.c has a heap-based buffer over-read.

tracker.name: CVE-2025-31344
tracker.description: Heap-based Buffer Overflow vulnerability in openEuler giflib on Linux. This vulnerability is associated with program files gif2rgb.C. This issue affects giflib: through 5.2.2.
tracker.source_text: CVE (at NVD; CERT, ENISA, LWN, oss-sec, fulldisc, Debian ELTS, Red Hat, Ubuntu, Gentoo, SUSE bugzilla/CVE, GitHub advisories/code/issues, web search, more)
notes: [bullseye] - giflib <postponed> (Minor issue, revisit when fixed upstream)

cna.title: The giflib open-source component has a buffer overflow vulnerability

tracker.name: CVE-2026-23868
tracker.description: Giflib contains a double-free vulnerability that is the result of a shallow copy in GifMakeSavedImage and incorrect error handling. The conditions needed to trigger this vulnerability are difficult but may be possible.
tracker.source_text: CVE (at NVD; CERT, ENISA, LWN, oss-sec, fulldisc, Debian ELTS, Red Hat, Ubuntu, Gentoo, SUSE bugzilla/CVE, GitHub advisories/code/issues, web search, more)

descriptions.value: Giflib contains a double-free vulnerability that is the result of a shallow copy in GifMakeSavedImage and incorrect error handling. The conditions needed to trigger this vulnerability are difficult but may be possible.

tracker.name: CVE-2026-26740
tracker.description: Buffer Overflow vulnerability in giflib v.5.2.2 allows a remote attacker to cause a denial of service via the EGifGCBToExtension overwriting an existing Graphic Control Extension block without validating its allocated size.
tracker.source_text: CVE (at NVD; CERT, ENISA, LWN, oss-sec, fulldisc, Debian ELTS, Red Hat, Ubuntu, Gentoo, SUSE bugzilla/CVE, GitHub advisories/code/issues, web search, more)

descriptions.value: Buffer Overflow vulnerability in giflib v.5.2.2 allows a remote attacker to cause a denial of service via the EGifGCBToExtension overwriting an existing Graphic Control Extension block without validating its allocated size.
